metadata.title: Cambridge Flyers Gold Mock 2
metadata.description: Manually authored A2 Flyers gold mock 2 — community, shopping and hobbies themes with open-ended writing and natural speaking prompts.
metadata.authoringNotes: M4.1 Gold Mock — manually authored and academically reviewed.

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Look at the pictures. Listen and draw a line from each name to the correct person.

This is Mrs Okonkwo. She's serving soup at the community stall.
Dylan's building a model aeroplane at the hobby club table.
Amira is arranging second-hand books on the charity sale table.
Mr Hughes fixes bicycles for the neighbourhood project every Saturday.
Sofia's got a colourful handmade bracelet she's selling at her craft stall.
Kai is standing beside a rack of football boots at the sports shop display.
The coordinator gave clear instructions to all the volunteers yesterday morning.
audio.caption: Link names to descriptions

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and write the missing word or number.

The shop closes at six o'clock on Saturdays.
The blue jacket in the window costs twenty-four pounds.
They need fifteen volunteers for the community garden project.
Bus number forty-two goes to the shopping centre every twenty minutes.
The chess club started in two thousand eighteen.
Ben swam the fastest length at the hobby club swimming session.
You need three eggs for the hobby club baking recipe.
audio.caption: Write words or numbers

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and Choose the correct answer.

The children are choosing fresh vegetables at the Saturday market.
In the music shop you can try different guitars before you buy one.
Last weekend volunteers cleaned litter from the park near the community centre.
The bookshop sells comics and magazines on Sundays.
The youth club is behind the library, next to the sports hall.
Tomorrow there will be a pottery demonstration at the community craft fair.
The handmade kite was the best hobby project and won first prize.
audio.caption: Choose the correct answer

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and colour or draw.

Colour the bakery sign golden yellow.
Draw a shopping trolley next to the supermarket entrance.
Colour the boy's hat purple.
Draw an arrow from the girl to the community centre.
Draw a skateboard on the pavement near the hobby shop.
Colour the awning above the café striped green and white.
Draw a dog sitting by the pet shop door.
audio.caption: Colour and draw

questions.questionText: Read: "Volunteers at the food bank are packing…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: Volunteers at the food bank are packing boxes of groceries for families in need.

questions.questionText: Read: "Shoppers are trying on winter coats on t…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: Shoppers are trying on winter coats on the third floor of the department store.

choices.text: People trying on coats in a shop

questions.questionText: Read: "Last month the drama club performed a pl…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: Last month the drama club performed a play about friendship at the community hall.

questions.questionText: Read: "The charity shop is going to sell handma…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: The charity shop is going to sell handmade gifts before the winter festival.

questions.questionText: Read: "The skate park has got new ramps and a s…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: The skate park has got new ramps and a safe area for beginners.

questions.questionText: Read: "While musicians were playing, families w…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: While musicians were playing, families were dancing in the street during the neighbourhood festival.

choices.text: Street festival with music and dancing

questions.questionText: Read: "Customers can compare prices online befo…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: Customers can compare prices online before they visit the high street shops.

choices.text: Person comparing prices on a laptop

questions.questionText:
Read the text beginning "Community Garden News

Last spring,…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage:
Community Garden News

Last spring, neighbours turned an empty plot into a shared garden. Tomatoes, herbs and sunflowers now grow in raised beds. Every Saturday, families water the plants and share gardening tips. Children painted colourful signs for each vegetable patch. Next month the garden club is going to organise a harvest picnic for the whole street.

choices.text: The garden is inside a shopping mall.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "Riverside Market opened in 1924 bes…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: Riverside Market opened in 1924 beside the old bridge. Traders sold fruit, fabric and tools to people from nearby villages. During the war the market closed for two years, but it reopened in 1946. Today you can buy street food, handmade jewellery and second-hand books. Many visitors say it is the liveliest place in town on Saturday mornings.

choices.text: The market reopened after the war.
No food is sold there now.

questions.questionText:
Read the text beginning "My Hobby Blog — Week 6

I am learni…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage:
My Hobby Blog — Week 6

I am learning to play the ukulele with my cousin. We are practising three songs for the school talent show. Our fingers hurt a little, but we are enjoying it. Tonight we are recording a short video to share with our grandparents. I think performing in front of people will be scary but exciting.

choices.text: The writer is learning to play the ukulele.
They have already won the talent show.

questions.questionText:
Read the text beginning "Youth Centre Notice

Members must b…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage:
Youth Centre Notice

Members must bring their membership card to every activity. You mustn't leave bags unattended in the changing rooms. The tuck shop sells healthy snacks and reusable water bottles. Profits help pay for new sports equipment. Next week the centre is going to open a swap shelf where you can exchange books and games.

choices.text: The tuck shop helps pay for sports equipment.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "While Lina was searching for a birt…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: While Lina was searching for a birthday present, the shop assistant was showing her different board games. Lina felt unsure because she wanted something special but not too expensive. She was comparing two puzzles when her brother called to say he preferred outdoor games. In the end she chose a kite kit and left the shop smiling.

choices.text: Lina bought a kite kit for a birthday present.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "The chess club meets on quiet after…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: The chess club meets on quiet afternoons, but the basketball club is louder and faster. Chess helps you plan carefully, while basketball improves your fitness. Some students think chess is more relaxing than basketball, but others say scoring a basket feels more exciting. The school encourages everyone to try both before choosing a favourite hobby.

choices.text: Some students prefer chess because it is more relaxing.
Basketball is always quieter than chess.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "The Spring Community Fair was the b…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: The Spring Community Fair was the busiest event of the year. The juggling workshop was the most popular activity for younger children. Food from local cafés was the tastiest part of the day, according to many visitors. Several people said the handmade craft stalls were the most colourful section. Everyone agreed the volunteer team did the best job organising the fair.

choices.text: Many visitors enjoyed the food from local cafés.

questions.questionText: Write a message inviting your friend to join a hobby club.

content.prompt: Write a message inviting your friend to join a hobby club you belong to. Explain why you think they would enjoy it and suggest a time to meet.
content.taskDescription: Invite your friend to a hobby club.
requiredPoints: Say which club you belong to
Explain why your friend might enjoy it
Suggest when and where to meet
Ask your friend to reply

questions.questionText: Write a thank-you note to volunteers at a community event.

content.prompt: Write a thank-you note to the volunteers who helped at a community event. Describe what they did and explain how their help made a difference.
requiredPoints: Say which event they helped with
Describe what the volunteers did
Explain why their help was important
Say something you hope happens next time

questions.questionText: Write a note recommending a shop to a classmate.

content.prompt: Write a note to a classmate recommending a shop you visited recently. Say what you bought and give your opinion about the shop.
requiredPoints: Say which shop you visited
Describe what you bought or saw
Give your opinion about the shop
Say whether you would go again

questions.questionText: Write a story called 'The Missing Wallet'.

content.prompt: Write a story called 'The Missing Wallet'. Include what happened at the market, how the characters felt, and how the problem was solved.
content.taskDescription: Write a story set at a market.
requiredPoints: Describe where the story happens
Include a problem and how characters felt
Explain how the problem was solved

questions.questionText: Write a story about discovering a new hobby.

content.prompt: Write a story about a child who discovers a new hobby. Describe what happened, how they felt at first, and what they learned.
content.taskDescription: Write a story about a new hobby.
requiredPoints: Say how the character discovered the hobby
Describe a challenge or surprise
Explain how the character felt at the end

questions.questionText: Read the scene below. Write about the busy market scene.

content.prompt: Read the scene below. Write three or four sentences about the busy market. Say what people are buying and what different stallholders are doing.
requiredPoints: Say what people are buying or selling
Describe at least two different activities in the picture
Use your own words to add a detail you notice

questions.questionText: Read the scene below. Write about the hobby room.

content.prompt: Read the scene below. Write three or four sentences about the hobby room. Compare two areas of the room and say what the people are doing.
requiredPoints: Compare two things in the picture
Say what at least two people are doing
Add one detail that shows why the room is special

questions.questionText: Read the two shop differences scenes below. Tell your partner about the differences.

content.prompt: Read the two descriptions of a shopping street. Tell your partner about five differences between them. Explain what you see and where things are.
content.taskDescription:
Scene A: A shopping street with a bookshop, a bakery with a long queue, and a bus stop.
Scene B: The same street with the bookshop closed, no queue at the bakery, and a taxi rank.
followUpQuestions: What is different about the bakery window?
How many shoppers can you see in Scene B?
Is there a bus in both scenes?
Where is the bicycle in Scene A?
What colour is the awning in Scene B?

questions.questionText: Answer the examiner's questions about about hobbies community.

content.prompt: The examiner will ask you about your hobbies, shopping habits and community activities. Answer naturally and give reasons for your opinions.
followUpQuestions: What hobby do you enjoy most and why?
Tell me about a shop you like visiting in your town.
Have you ever helped at a community event? What did you do?
What new hobby would you like to try and why?
How do you usually decide what to buy when you go shopping?

questions.questionText: Use the story outline to tell the story of a family visit to the community fair. Describe what happened, how people felt, and how the day ended.

content.prompt: Use the story outline to tell the story of a family visit to the community fair. Describe what happened, how people felt, and how the day ended.
content.taskDescription:
Story outline:
1. A family arrives at the community fair.
2. They visit a hobby stall and try a craft activity.
3. The youngest child gets lost for a short time.
4. They find each other and leave the fair happy.
followUpQuestions: Why did the family go to the fair?
What did they buy or try at the hobby stall?
What problem happened in the third picture?
How did they solve the problem?
How did everyone feel at the end of the day?

coverageAchieved.notes: Authored for M3.1 Gold Mock specification.
